data.title: Invitation to Ferronordic's investor presentation 20 February 2025
data.description:
Ferronordic AB

Ferronordic's year-end report for 2024 will be published on 20 February 2025 at 07:30 CET. A presentation of the report will be held the same day at 10:00 CET.

Ferronordic’s year-end report for 2024 will be published on 20 February 2025 at 07.30 CET and will be accessible at www.ferronordic.com. Ferronordic invites investors, analysts and the media to a presentation where Lars Corneliusson, CEO, and Erik Danemar, CFO, will comment on the report. The

presentation will be held on 20 February 2025 at 10:00 CET and can be followed via telephone conference or webcast.

The presentation will be held in English and will be followed by a questions and answers session. Questions

can be asked via the telephone conference or in written form via the webcast.

If you wish to participate via teleconference, please register on the link below. After registration you will be provided phone numbers and a conference ID to access the conference.

https://conference.inderes.com/teleconference/?id=5007843

If you wish to participate via webcast, please use the link below.

https://ferronordic.events.inderes.com/q4-report-2024

After the presentation, a recording will be available at the same web page.

 

-------------------------------------------------------

 

 

About Ferronordic

Ferronordic is a service and sales company in the areas of construction equipment and trucks. It is the dealer for Volvo CE in all or parts of nine states in the United States and also represents Hitachi, Sandvik and Link-Belt in parts of the same area. Ferronordic is dealer of Volvo Trucks and Renault Trucks in Germany and dealer of Volvo CE, Sandvik and certain other brands in Kazakhstan. Ferronordic began its operations in 2010 and currently has 41 outlets and approx. 800 employees. Ferronordic’s vision is to be the leading service and sales company in its markets. The shares in Ferronordic AB (publ) are listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. www.ferronordic.com

The information was submitted for publication on 6 February 2025, 12:30 CET. 

Contact

For investors, analysts and media:

Erik Danemar, CFO and Head of Investor Relations

+46 73 660 72 31

ir@ferronordic.com

https://news.cision.com/ferronordic-ab/r/invitation-to-ferronordic-s-investor-presentation-20-february-2025%2Cc4101581

Press release - Invitation to Ferronordic's investor presentation 20 February 2025

data.title: Linde sees slower 1st quarter earnings on unfavourable currency effect
data.description:
Linde , the world's largest industrial gases company, sees slightly slower earnings growth in the first quarter of 2025, citing unfavourable currency exchange effects.

The U.S.-German company, which supplies gases such as oxygen, nitrogen and hydrogen to factories and hospitals, expects the first quarter adjusted earnings per share to be in a range of $3.85 to $3.95, a 3% to 5% rise from the same period last year.

"As anticipated, global macro conditions have continued to weaken, especially foreign currency translation," CEO Sanjiv Lamba said in a statement.

Linde is seen as a bellwether for industrial production as it supplies gases for a range of customers in sectors including chemicals, manufacturing, steel-making, and food and beverages.

This diversified footprint can help the company cope with weakness in some end markets, as others can compensate for it. Linde has regularly beaten earnings estimates or raised its guidance in recent years.

The Woking Surrey-based company reported an 11% rise in its fourth quarter adjusted earnings per share to $3.97, ahead of analysts' mean estimate of $3.93 per share in an LSEG poll.

data.title: LG Uplus Swings to Q4 2024 Loss as Sales Slide 2%
data.description:
LG Uplus swung to an attributable loss of 19.5 billion won in the fourth quarter of 2024 from a profit of 100.9 billion won in the year-ago period.

The South Korean mobile network operator's sales fell 1.8% to 3.753 trillion won from 3.821 trillion won, according to a Thursday filing with the country's Financial Supervisory Service.

data.title: Prestige Consumer Healthcare Fiscal Q3 Adjusted EPS, Revenue Increase; Shares Rise Pre-Bell
data.description:
Prestige Consumer Healthcare reported fiscal Q3 adjusted earnings Thursday of $1.22 per diluted share, up from $1.06 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $1.16.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $290.3 million, up from $282.7 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $286.5 million.

The company now expects fiscal 2025 adjusted diluted EPS of about $4.50 and revenue between $1.128 billion and $1.132 billion.

The previous guidance was adjusted diluted EPS between $4.40 and $4.46 and revenue between $1.125 billion and $1.140 billion.

Analysts polled by FactSet expect $4.44 and $1.13 billion.

The company's shares were rising 3.8% in recent premarket activity.

data.title: Northamber Unit Buys AV Distributor Epatra
data.description:
Northamber said Thursday that its subsidiary Northamber NL acquired Epatra, a distributor of professional audiovisual products based in the Netherlands from PreHold and Lumen Vera as part of a share purchase agreement.

The total consideration for the deal was up to 2 million euros, with potential payments of up to 850,000 euros over the next three to five years contingent on the target's future financial performance.

The audio-visual and information technology group also assumed 1.22 million euros in existing debt, repayable to the sellers in three equal tranches. The sellers will also provide transitional services to Epatra for up to 36 months.

The deal aligns with Northamber's plans to expand its European presence beyond the UK and enables consolidation of its Benelux operations.

data.title: What’s the top source of stress for older workers? New HOOPP research suggests people are more worried about their finances than any other issue in their lives
data.description:
TORONTO, Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — New research conducted for the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan (HOOPP) indicates the top source of stress for workers aged 40 to 60 is their financial well-being – and for most people, it’s a daily concern.

The findings are based on a study of more than 2,800 participants who were asked to identify the biggest sources of stress in their lives: 58 per cent of respondents listed financial stress as their top worry. Health concerns (39 per cent), worries about relationships or family issues (37 per cent) and work or school-related stress (37 per cent) were also cited. Additionally, the study found that 60 per cent of the participants thought about their current financial situation daily or weekly.

The study group was made up of full-time workers between the ages of 40 and 60 who are members of a defined benefit (DB) pension plan. Researchers also collected data from a separate group of workers aged 40-60 who do not have a workplace pension. The workers with a DB pension plan reported a 9 per cent higher financial well-being and 10 per cent lower financial stress than those without a pension.

“Stress – including stress about your financial situation – is a key factor influencing both physical and mental health,” said Ivana Zanardo, HOOPP’s Head of Plan Services. “This new research also suggests women in the workplace are experiencing higher levels of financial stress and lower levels of financial well-being than men.”

The report defines financial well-being as a person’s ability to meet their financial obligations, feel secure in their financial future and make choices that allow them to enjoy life. Financial stress refers to psychological strain associated with managing one’s finances, such as worrying about meeting financial obligations, income security, challenges with savings or accumulating debt.

Zanardo added, “The economic benefits of having a pension are well known and contribute to financial security in retirement. There is growing evidence based on research by academics from around the world that financial stress can lead to serious health problems, but having a pension can reduce that stress. HOOPP commissioned this research to better understand how pensions contribute positively to our members’ health and well-being.”

This new study – conducted for HOOPP by the Behavioural Insights Team, a global research firm – also includes a review of academic research that looks at the effects of having a pension on health and well-being. These new findings follow other HOOPP research from the last year which shows, among other things, a majority of Canadians feel financially unprepared for retirement.

About the Healthcare of Ontario Pension Plan

HOOPP serves Ontario’s hospital and community-based healthcare sector, with more than 700 participating employers. Its membership includes nurses, medical technicians, food services staff, housekeeping staff, and many others who provide valued healthcare services. In total, HOOPP has more than 475,000 active, deferred and retired members.

HOOPP operates as a private independent trust, and its Board of Trustees governs the Plan and Fund, focusing on HOOPP’s mission to deliver on our pension promise. The Board is made up of appointees from the Ontario Hospital Association (OHA) and four unions: the Ontario Nurses’ Association (ONA), the Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE), the Ontario Public Service Employees' Union (OPSEU), and the Service Employees International Union (SEIU). This governance model provides representation from both employers and members in support of the long-term interests of the Plan.

Contact:

Scott White, Senior Director, Media Relations & External Communications

swhite2@hoopp.com

data.title: German fiscal easing scenarios: modest lift or boost?
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 hits record, up 0.7%
  • Frankfurt and London also hit peaks
  • SocGen, AstraZeneca rise on earnings
  • BoE set to cut rates
  • Wall St futures edge higher

GERMAN FISCAL EASING SCENARIOS: MODEST LIFT OR BOOST?

A post-election boost to German fiscal spending, namely though the reform of the debt-brake rule, is top of mind of investors buying into European stocks these days.

UBS has three potential scenarios which it has been discussing with clients ahead of the vote on February 23.

The Swiss bank's base-case to which it gives a 60% probability is of a modest cyclical lift.

Under this option, a debt brake reform would lead to a 0.7% of GDP in extra spending, driving more funds on defence and just a modest extra support for households and corporates.

"Growth could rise back to its potential level of 0.8% by 2026, but structural headwinds would remain," writes UBS economist Felix Huefner.

The best-case but less likely scenario (15% chance) sees substantially higher fiscal spending and a structural reform agenda that raises Germany's medium-term potential growth rate.

Under their third and last scenario - of an ongoing stalemate, likely because the two-third majority needed for reform is not reached - the growth outlook for Europe's No.1 economy could become weaker than UBS currently expects.

Current polls, UBS notes, suggest that the CDU/CSU, SPD  and Greens could end up with 71% of seats in parliament, leaving the far-right and far-left parties with no veto power.

*****

data.title: Suncor Energy Outperform Rating, $68 Price Target Reiterated by National Bank of Canada After Q4 Results
data.description:
Suncor Energy had its Outperform stock rating and $68 price target maintained by National Bank of Canada after the company reported fourth-quarter 2024 earnings after market hours on Wednesday.

Suncor reported a slight beat versus consensus cash flow per share estimates at $2.78. National Bank's estimate was $2.67 and the analyst consensus estimate was $2.63.

Average production of 875,000 barrels per day, recording an 8% increase, was in line with both National Bank's forecast of 873,000 b/d and the Street at 874,000 b/d.

Capital spending was $1.5 billion, excluding capitalized interest, which compared to both the bank's estimates and the Street at $1.65 billion.

data.title: GUIDANCE: (HAE) Haemonetics Expects Fiscal 2025 Adjusted EPS Range $4.50 - $4.70, vs. FactSet Est of $4.35
RA International to Delist from London's AIM
data.description:
RA International Group said Thursday it plans to delist from the London bourse's AIM segment following a review.

The proposal is subject to the approval of shareholders at a general meeting scheduled for Feb. 28. If approved, the remote site construction group's delisting will take effect March 11.

data.title: Haemonetics Corporation Reports Third Quarter and Year-to-Date Fiscal 2025 Results
data.description:
Haemonetics Corporation, a global healthcare company specializing in innovative medical products and solutions, has released its financial results for the third quarter and year-to-date fiscal 2025, ending December 28, 2024. The company also provided updated guidance for fiscal 2025.

Financial Highlights

Haemonetics reported a third-quarter revenue of $348.5 million, marking a 3.7% increase compared to the same period in fiscal 2024. Year-to-date revenue reached $1,030 million, up 6.7% from the previous year. Earnings per diluted share (EPS) for the third quarter were $0.74, while adjusted EPS stood at $1.19. The company generated $44 million in cash flow from operating activities and $30 million in free cash flow during the quarter.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company's business units showed mixed performance. The Plasma segment saw a decline of 9.1% in revenue, while the Blood Center segment decreased by 2.8%. However, the Hospital segment experienced significant growth, with a 23.9% increase in revenue. Gross margin improved to 55.5% from 52.9% in the same quarter last year, driven by volume growth in higher-margin products and pricing benefits across all business units.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Haemonetics made notable strides in its strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of Advanced Cooling Therapy, Inc. (d/b/a Attune Medical) in early fiscal 2025. The company also engaged in share repurchases and debt financing activities, which contributed to an increase in cash on hand to $320.8 million as of December 28, 2024.

Management's Perspective

Chris Simon, CEO of Haemonetics, commented on the results, stating, "Our record third-quarter margins demonstrate our long-range plan is successfully driving profitable growth. We are gaining share, driving meaningful value through our differentiated technologies, and strengthening our leadership as we navigate external market challenges, while accelerating portfolio evolution for further margin expansion. We are confident in the foundation we are building for sustained long-term growth across our business."

Future Outlook

The company updated its fiscal 2025 guidance, projecting a total revenue growth of 3-5% and organic revenue growth of 0-3%. Adjusted operating margin is expected to be around 24%, with adjusted earnings per diluted share ranging from $4.50 to $4.70. Free cash flow guidance was revised to $120 million to $140 million.

SEC Filing: HAEMONETICS CORP [ HAE ] - 8-K - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: Entegris Reports Strong Q4 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
Entegris, Inc., a leading supplier of advanced materials and process solutions for the semiconductor and other high-tech industries, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. The company reported robust performance, with significant growth in key financial metrics.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter ended December 31, 2024, Entegris reported net sales of $850 million, marking a 5% increase from the prior year. Adjusted net sales, which exclude the impact of divestitures, rose by 11% year-over-year. The company achieved a GAAP diluted EPS of $0.67 and a non-GAAP diluted EPS of $0.84.

Entegris' gross margin as a percentage of net sales improved to 45.6%, up from 42.4% in the same period last year. The operating margin also saw a significant increase, reaching 17.6% compared to 12.4% in the previous year. Net income for the quarter was $102.2 million, a substantial rise from $38.0 million in Q4 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

The company operates in two segments: Materials Solutions (MS) and Advanced Purity Solutions (APS). The MS segment reported net sales of $361.1 million, while the APS segment achieved $491.2 million in net sales. Both segments contributed to the overall growth, with APS showing a notable increase from the previous year.

Entegris continues to focus on advanced logic and AI-driven applications, although visibility outside these areas remains limited. The company is committed to delivering strong market outperformance and profitability, improving free cash flow, and funding critical investments to enhance long-term competitiveness.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Entegris has been actively managing its debt, with long-term debt reduced to $3.98 billion from $4.58 billion at the end of 2023. The company also reported cash and cash equivalents of $329.2 million, down from $456.9 million in the previous year, reflecting its strategic investments and debt management efforts.

Additionally, Entegris has been involved in restructuring activities aimed at cost savings, which have resulted in charges of $3.9 million for the quarter. The company also completed the sale of certain businesses, contributing to its financial performance.

Management's Perspective

Bertrand Loy, President and CEO of Entegris, expressed confidence in the company's performance and future prospects. He highlighted the strong performance in Q4 2024, exceeding guidance for both sales and non-GAAP EPS. Loy emphasized the company's focus on advanced logic and AI-driven applications, while acknowledging the limited visibility in other areas of the semiconductor market.

Loy also reiterated the company's commitment to improving free cash flow, funding critical investments, and positioning Entegris for future industry upturns. He expressed confidence in the long-term growth outlook of the semiconductor industry and the opportunities it presents for Entegris.

Future Outlook

For the first quarter ending March 29, 2025, Entegris expects sales to range between $775 million and $805 million, representing a 7% year-on-year increase, excluding the impact of divestitures. The company anticipates GAAP net income of $58 million to $68 million, with diluted earnings per common share expected to be between $0.38 and $0.45. On a non-GAAP basis, diluted earnings per common share are projected to range from $0.64 to $0.71.

Entegris also expects adjusted EBITDA to be approximately 28.0% to 29.0% of sales for the first quarter of 2025, reflecting its ongoing efforts to maintain strong financial performance and market outperformance.

SEC Filing: ENTEGRIS INC [ ENTG ] - 8-K - Feb. 06, 2025

data.title: Street View: Disney's upcoming streaming content to boost subscriber growth
data.description:
** Walt Disney sharply outperformed Wall Street's quarterly earnings estimates on Wednesday, thanks to strong holiday box office performance of animated sequel "Moana 2," though the company warned of a modest decline in Disney+ streaming subscribers in the coming quarter

** Median PT of 37 brokerages covering the stock is $126.1, an upside of 14.3% to the current price of $110.31 - data compiled by LSEG

FOCUS ON DISNEY+ SUBSCRIBER GROWTH

** TD Cowen ("hold," PT: $123) says DIS is well-managed and has valuable content and believes Disney+ is doing very well, which is crucial for the stock's performance

** Company will eventually recover from pandemic-related challenges, but there are concerns about its long-term strategy, especially the Fox acquisition, says TD Cowen

** J.P.Morgan("overweight," PT: $130) says DIS appears more confident in harnessing its content and a better app experience to boost user engagement and reduce cancellations

** Jefferies ("hold," PT: $120) says price hikes and exiting a wholesale deal will lead to continued losses in Q2, but believes streaming new movies such as "Moana 2" and "Mufasa" will boost subscriber growth, despite cost cuts leading to inconsistent releases

** Moffett Nathanson ("buy," PT: $140) says DIS will eventually create a more unified direct-to-consumer (DTC) strategy, which will become profitable over time, "but understand this will continue to be more of a show-me story"

data.title: Hannover Re expects €500mn-€700mn CA wildfire loss but FY guidance remains unchanged
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Hannover Re has disclosed that it is anticipating a loss of €500mn to €700mn ($518mn-$725mn) from the Los Angeles wildfires, although its full-year guidance remains unchanged despite the event exceeding the reinsurer's Q1 catastrophe budget.

Speaking on a media call on Thursday morning, CEO Jean-Jacques Henchoz said the estimate was based on an industry loss of between $30bn and $40bn from the wildfires.

He added that this estimate – which is based on internal modelling and scenario analysis, not on a detailed bottom-up estimate – would exceed Hannover Re’s large loss budget of €435mn for Q1 2024.

“The remaining large loss budget for the full year and our balance sheet strength will enable us to absorb this loss and meet our guidance for the full year, which remains unchanged,” Henchoz continued.

Late last year, Hannover Re confirmed that it expected a P&C reinsurance combined ratio under 88 percent in 2025, assuming large losses do not significantly exceed the budgeted €2.1bn.

“Each time you have a major nat cat event, the question of public sector intervention comes in. I think there is no one-size-fits-all approach to that problem. Pools, in some instances, can be part of the response to those huge nat cat exposures,” Henchoz continued.

“I think generally the issue which comes back again and again with such an event – and in full force in California – is that climate change needs to be tackled from the point of view of not only mitigation strategies, but also adaptation strategies.”

In the case of wildfires, mitigation and adaptation measures include building codes, distancing and preventive measures against fire.

“If you put together a public sector pool, then you might have insurance capacity available, but the question remains at what price. If the price of insurance coverage is determined by the public sector and not by the risk information and modelling, then you basically have subsidised insurance, which is not solving the problem in the end,” said Henchoz.

“So pools are possibly a part of a response for difficult-to-insure risk, but I think we have to put this in a much broader context and tackle the big topic of climate change adaptation.”

Sven Althoff, the member of Hannover Re's executive board responsible for P&C reinsurance, added during the call that the California wildfires will likely stabilise terms and conditions over 2025.

“The wildfires in Los Angeles have highlighted that the adequate pricing of the business and adequate terms and conditions continue to be indispensable for sustained reinsurance protection,” said Althoff.

“When it comes to forthcoming renewals in California, of course the loss will be taken into account, so it is fair to assume that the loss experience from the wildfires will at a minimum trigger rate and premium increases. In some instances, it may also trigger increases in the retention levels of our ceding companies.”

1.1.25 renewals

Speaking on the recent 1 January renewals more broadly, Henchoz said the renewal season was characterised by increased reinsurance supply, mainly driven by retained earnings of incumbent reinsurers.

“Growth in supply exceeded the growth in demand, which was less pronounced than anticipated. As a result, overall, reinsurance prices in January were slightly decreasing, particularly in the nat cat business,” said Henchoz.

“However, reinsurance structures, as well as retention levels and terms and conditions, remained broadly stable during this renewal.”

He continued that the 1.1 renewals were generally successful for Hannover Re, which reported premium growth of 7.6 percent, with risk-adjusted pricing down 2.1 percent across its portfolio.

“While the price competition in this round of renewals was a bit more notable in some areas than we had anticipated, we can look back on successful renewals in a market that remains attractive.”

Hannover Re disclosed that €950mn of premiums were non-renewed at 1.1, which Althoff said was driven by a combination of classes of business.

“This was sometimes driven by us as we were no longer happy with the profitability levels of the business. For example, the agricultural business in China would be an area,” he said.

“Sometimes it was also driven by the ceding companies, which restructured from pro rata to excess of loss business – some of the German business can be mentioned here. So there was some property-related business and nat cat-related business in the €950mn, but it was not the main driver for this development.”

Althoff concluded that reinsurance pricing remains on a risk-adequate level, despite some price reductions on loss-free programs.

“On the other hand, we also still saw increases where we had experienced losses during the year 2024. The quality of our diversified portfolio, coupled with sustained increased demand, gives us confidence as we look ahead to the further renewals during the year,” he said.

data.title: North Macedonia Trade Deficit Widens in December
data.description:
North Macedonia’s trade deficit widened to $388 million in December 2024 from $329 million in the corresponding month of the previous year.

Exports shrank 17.5% year-on-year to $592.5 million, while imports declined at a much softer pace of 6.3% to $980.9 million.

Considering the whole period of 2024, exports decreased 6.4% compared to the same period a year earlier to $8,425.3 million, weighed down by lower sales of mineral fuels, lubricants and related materials (-21.2%) and chemicals and related products, n.e.s. (-20.1%).

Meanwhile, imports declined at a slower 0.7% to $11,969.6 million, primarily due to reduced purchases of crude materials, inedible, except fuels (-11.%), mineral fuels, lubricants and related materials (-10.2%), and chemicals and related products, n.e.s. (-4.1%).

However, purchases accelerated for beverages and tobacco (16.4%), animal and vegetable oils, fats and waxes (11.7%), and machinery and transport equipment (8.5%).

data.title: Sarajevo bourse turnover drops below 1 mln euro in Jan
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), February 6 (SeeNews) - Bosnia and Herzegovina’s Sarajevo Stock Exchange (SASE) said it recorded a 1.9 million marka ($1 million/971,454 euro) turnover in January, dropping from 252.2 million marka in December, when the bourse organised three government bond auctions.

In January, 129,843 shares were traded in 315 transactions on SASE, the bourse said in a monthly report on Wednesday.

The BIFX, the SASE index that follows the performance of investment funds, lost 2.72% in January and closed at 529.57 points.

The SASX-10 index, which tracks the top 10 companies in terms of market cap and trading frequency, gained 1.87% compared to December and closed at 1,254.46 points. The broader SASX-30 index, which contains the 30 most liquid stocks on the bourse, rose 0.83% to 2,230.87 points at the end of January.

The Islamic stock index SASX-BBI, jointly managed by the bourse and Bosna Bank International (BBI), closed January at 13,070.07 points, marking a 1.51% increase.

In January, Federation-owned BH Telecom [SAJ:BHTS] shares generated the highest turnover of 562,574 marka in 135 transactions. The company’s shares climbed 12.18% to 15.66 marka. The company recently announced that it plans to acquire local peer Telemach and its business in Montenegro from United Group.

SASE is based in the Federation, one of two autonomous entities that form Bosnia and Herzegovina. The country's other bourse, the Banja Luka Stock Exchange (BLSE), is based in the other entity, the Serb Republic.

(1 euro = 1.956 marka)

data.title: TrialWire Platform AI Voice Screen to Boost Patient Engagement by 30% - Launched at Biotech Showcase 2025
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO, Feb. 06, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — TrialWire, a leader in clinical trial recruitment innovation, launched its groundbreaking AI Voice Screen service as part of the TrialWire Recruitment Platform powered by Salesforce Health Cloud, during the recent Biotech Showcase event in San Francisco.

Designed to capture an estimated 30% of potential patients who are missing due to screening communication challenges, the AI Voice Screen sets a new benchmark in managing medical literacy, language, and engagement.

For example, more than 50% of people don’t know the names of the medications they are on, and another 20% only know them by the size, color, and shape of tablets. The AI Voice Screener can wait while they check their scripts or even use image recognition.

This state-of-the-art solution uses advanced AI language models to streamline and optimize patient engagement and screening processes for clinical trials.

Key Features of the TrialWire AI Voice Screen Service:

  • Across all Languages and Medical Literacy:The AI-powered voice screening tool works across all languages and accommodates all medical literacy levels, improving screen success and ultimately bringing more people of diverse backgrounds, including age and demographics, into trials.
  • Enhanced Patient Engagement:The AI-powered voice screening tool facilitates personalized and real-time interactions with potential clinical trial participants. By leveraging natural language processing, the system accurately captures patient information and eligibility details, ensuring a seamless and efficient initial screening process.
  • Efficiency and Accuracy:This innovation not only accelerates the recruitment process but also minimizes screen fail rates and improves the accuracy of data collection for sites.
  • Scalability and Integration:TrialWire’s solution integrates effortlessly with the TrialWire Platform. Its scalable design accommodates clinical trials of any size, from early-phase studies to large-scale global trials, ensuring broad applicability across the industry.
  • Data Security and Compliance:Recognizing the importance of data privacy and security, the AI Voice Screen service adheres to strict regulatory standards, including HIPAA and GDPR compliance. TrialWire is committed to protecting patient information while delivering cutting-edge technology.

“The launch of our AI Voice Screen service represents a real step forward for clinical trial recruitment,” said Susan Fitzpatrick-Napier Founder and CEO at TrialWire.

“By leveraging advanced AI technologies, we are streamlining the screening process by creating a more engaging and accessible experience for patients.

“We were honored to be invited to present this innovation at Biotech Showcase and look forward to its positive impact on clinical trial efficiency and patient outcomes.”

About TrialWire:

TrialWire is at the forefront of digital innovation in clinical trial recruitment and management. Committed to improving patient access and engagement in clinical research, TrialWire combines cutting-edge technology with industry expertise to deliver solutions that enhance efficiency, data quality, and overall patient experience. For more information about the TrialWire Platform and its suite of services, visit Trial-Wire.com.

About Biotech Showcase:

Biotech Showcase is a premier event that brings together investors, innovators, and thought leaders in the biotechnology sector. The event serves as a platform for showcasing the latest breakthroughs, fostering collaborations, and driving the future of healthcare innovation.

Media Contact:

For interviews and more information:

data.title: Honeywell International's Q4 Adjusted Earnings Fall as Net Sales Rise; Sets 2025 Guidance
data.description:
Honeywell International reported Q4 adjusted earnings Thursday of $2.47 per diluted share, down from $2.69 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $2.32.

Net sales for the quarter ended Dec. 31 were $10.09 billion, compared with $9.44 billion a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $9.84 billion.

Honeywell expects full-year 2025 adjusted EPS to be between $10.10 and 10.50. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect $10.92.

The company expects 2025 sales of $39.6 billion to $40.6 billion, while analysts polled by FactSet expect $41.26 billion.

Honeywell shares were down 5% in recent pre-market activity.

data.title: IT stocks lift Sri Lankan shares higher
data.description:
Sri Lankan shares closed higher on Thursday, aided by gains in information technology stocks.

* The CSE All Share index (.CSE) settled up 0.31% at 16,506.73.

* UB Finance Company and Sigiriya Village Hotels were the top percentage gainers on the CSE All Share, rising 14.3% and 10.7%, respectively.

* Trading volume on the CSE All Share index fell to 177.1 million shares from 241.3 million in the previous session.

* The equity market's turnover dropped to 5.18 billion Sri Lankan rupees ($17.4 million) from 6.35 billion rupees in the previous session, according to exchange data.

* Foreign investors were net sellers, offloading stocks worth 489.8 million rupees, while domestic investors were net buyers, purchasing shares worth 4.82 billion rupees, the data showed.

* For a report on global markets, click

($1 = 298.1000 Sri Lankan rupees)

data.title: Godawari Power & Ispat's Steel Billets Approved for PGCIL Transmission Projects
data.description:
Godawari Power & Ispat said Power Grid Corporation of India has approved its steel billets for use in galvanized steel structures for transmission projects, according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

The approval makes Godawari Power eligible to supply billets to manufacturers of such structures. The company, which previously procured billets from large steel makers, expects cost savings as it shifts to using its own production, according to the filing.
